meaning: adj.不能做(或干)的,做(或干)不了的
examples: And the best thing is that refactorings are undoable.
Commands are used to perform all of the user's undoable changes.
VMware has three different disk modes: persistent, doable and undoable.
Others are in bad financial shape or must compete in unforgiving markets where price hikes are all but undoable.
At issue in the meeting was how to restart formal talks on Clinton's budget, which Republicans have rejected as undoable.
